2603.26246.title: Distilling Conversations: Abstract Compression of Conversational Audio Context for LLM-based ASR
2603.26246.paper_summary: Standard LLM-based speech recognition systems typically process utterances in isolation, limiting their ability to leverage conversational context. In this work, we study whether multimodal context from prior turns improves LLM-based ASR and how to represent that context efficiently. We find that, after supervised multi-turn training, conversational context mainly helps with the recognition of contextual entities. However, conditioning on raw context is expensive because the prior-turn audio token sequence grows rapidly with conversation length. To address this, we propose Abstract Compression, which replaces the audio portion of prior turns with a fixed number of learned latent tokens while retaining corresponding transcripts explicitly. On both in-domain and out-of-domain test sets, the compressed model recovers part of the gains of raw-context conditioning with a smaller prior-turn audio footprint. We also provide targeted analyses of the compression setup and its trade-offs.
2603.26246.paper_summary_zh: 標準的基於 LLM 的語音識別系統通常獨立處理語句，限制了它們利用對話上下文的能力。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們研究了來自先前回合的多模態上下文是否改善基於 LLM 的 ASR，以及如何有效地表示該上下文。<lb/>我們發現，在經過監督的多回合訓練後，對話上下文主要有助於對上下文實體的識別。<lb/>然而，基於原始上下文的條件化是昂貴的，因為先前回合的音頻標記序列會隨著對話長度迅速增長。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了抽象壓縮，這種方法用固定數量的學習潛在標記替換先前回合的音頻部分，同時明確保留相應的文字記錄。<lb/>在內部和外部測試集上，壓縮模型在較小的先前回合音頻佔用下恢復了原始上下文條件化的一部分增益。<lb/>我們還提供了對壓縮設置及其權衡的針對性分析。
2603.26246.author: Shashi Kumar et.al.
2603.26246.authors: Shashi Kumar, Esaú Villatoro-Tello, Sergio Burdisso, Kadri Hacioglu, Thibault Bañeras-Roux, Hasindri Watawana, Dairazalia Sanchez-Cortes, Srikanth Madikeri, Petr Motlicek, Andreas Stolcke
